prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in this year of our Lord, Two Thousand and Twenty-Three, didst the mighty software as a service company proclaim their third-quarter results. Lo, their annual recurring revenue didst ascend unto the heavens, reaching a sum of sixteen million pounds sterling. Alas, their operating loss didst swell like a tempestous sea, amounting to five million pounds sterling. The company didst herald the acquisition of a new treasure, known as ‘Acme Software', whose value didst stagger the mind at nineteen million pounds sterling.
